name: Coto Cuadros and Santomera Reservoir Loop
name_original: Torre vigilancia Cotocuadros y Embalse De Santomera – Ruta Circular
description: Mountain bike this moderate 21.3-mile loop around Santomera Dam, featuring diverse terrain, the Coto Cuadros Watchtower, and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Molina de Segura offers diverse terrain, from agricultural plains along the Segura River to more challenging hilly areas. The region is characterized by gentle mountains, natural wetlands, and reservoirs, providing varied landscapes for riders. This blend of riverine paths, elevated viewpoints, and dedicated trails caters to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Molina De Segura The most popular mountain bike trail is Coto Cuadros and Santomera Reservoir Loop, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Coto Cuadros area and passes by the Santomera Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ricote Valley – Mirador del Solvente loop from Archena-Fortuna, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.1 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from Mirador del Solvente within the Ricote Valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Lila Bridge loop from Molina de Segura, a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail leading through riverside areas, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Molina de Segura is defined by riverine paths, agricultural plains, and hilly terrains, including routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Molina de Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Molina de Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Molina de Segura?
A: Molina de Segura offers a network of nearly 30 mountain bike trails. These include a good mix of difficulties, with 5 easy routes, 19 moderate routes, and 5 challenging options for more experienced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Molina de Segura?
A: The terrain around Molina de Segura is quite diverse. You'll find routes traversing agricultural plains and Mediterranean fruit plantations along the Segura River, as well as more challenging hilly and gentle mountainous areas. Some trails also explore natural wetlands and pass by reservoirs like the Embalse de Santomera.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Molina de Segura?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14633237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lila Bridge loop from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 24.9 km trail that leads through riverside areas, offering a pleasant ride for various skill levels.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking here?
A: You can encounter a variety of natural features. Routes often follow the Segura River, and you might pass by reservoirs such as the Embalse de Santomera. The region also features unique desert landscapes, particularly on the road to Los Baños de Fortuna, with formations shaped by ancient seas. Highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Abanilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3152197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38269713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley – El Gurugú loop from Archena-Fortuna&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over its 56.7 km distance. Additionally, the Las Salinas area in Molina de Segura is known for its downhill circuit, specifically designed for enduro mountain biking.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Molina de Segura?
A: The mountain bike trails in Molina de Segura are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes from riverine paths to hilly viewpoints.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6892237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley – Mirador del Solvente loop from Archena-Fortuna&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.1 km path specifically noted for its panoramic views from Mirador del Solvente within the Ricote Valley. You might also find great vistas from higher terrains on routes like the Casa Calarria loop.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Molina de Segura are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921195130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Cuadros and Santomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14633237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lila Bridge loop from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38284285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Rojo – Riverside Birch Grove loop from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Molina de Segura?
A: The Murcia region, where Molina de Segura is located, generally enjoys a mild climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal for mountain biking, offering comfortable temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during this season.

Q: Are there any routes that follow a Greenway (Vía Verde)?
A: Yes, the region features sections of Greenways. The "Vía Verde del Noroeste" is a popular choice, with routes like the "Embalse de los Rodeos y Antigua estacion por la Via Verde del Noroeste" offering a moderate ride along this converted railway line.

Q: Are there any specific areas for downhill or enduro mountain biking?
A: For those interested in downhill or enduro mountain biking, the area of Las Salinas in Molina de Segura is specifically highlighted as having a downhill circuit designed for this style of riding.

Q: What are some longer mountain bike routes available from Molina de Segura?
A: While many routes are day trips, it's possible to undertake longer mountain biking journeys starting from Molina de Segura. For instance, you can embark on a significant 160 km route that follows the Segura River all the way to Guardamar del Segura.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 34.3 km (21.3 miles) long and generally takes around 3 hours and 10 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate for mountain biking. While much of it is accessible, some sections can be technical, requiring a basic understanding of enduro techniques. Expect a mix of surfaces and some challenging parts, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain, from good sandy paths to trails full of stones and steps. The area can be very dry, but after rains, certain sections, particularly in ravines and near the riverbed, can become technical with mud and water. There might even be short un-ridable sections where you'd need to dismount.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular views and passes by significant landmarks. Key highlights include the Santomera Dam, which the trail circles, and the historic Coto Cuadros Watchtower, offering panoramic vistas. The area around Embalse de Santomera is known as a 'trail paradise'.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the dry conditions typical of the Murcia region, it's generally best to ride during cooler months, such as autumn, winter, or spring, to avoid extreme heat. Be aware that after heavy rains, some technical sections can become muddy and more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the route is a circular one around the Embalse de Santomera and through Coto Cuadros. You can typically find suitable parking near the start of the loop in the vicinity of the Santomera Dam or the Coto Cuadros area.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this trail is not available. However, when riding with dogs in natural areas, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially around wildlife and other trail users, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this specific mountain biking route or the general Coto Cuadros area. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is there a water source available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail research mentions a 'water source' located within the Coto Cuadros area. Nevertheless, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water, especially given the potentially dry conditions in Murcia.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 13% of this circular route overlaps with sections of the official Transmurciana 27 trail, offering a chance to experience a part of this wider network.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding weather conditions?
faqs.answer: The region can be very dry, so sun protection and hydration are crucial. Conversely, after rains, the terrain can become significantly more technical, with many stones, steps, and mud in ravines and near the riverbed. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.
